title: lycopodiella caroliniana var. grandifolia
intro: Lycopodiella caroliniana var. grandifolia, also known as the large-leaved club moss, is a type of evergreen, perennial plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae and is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Lycopodiella caroliniana var. grandifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and potentially treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be made by drying and processing the plant's leaves and stems.
